skill.name: planning-with-files
skill.description: Implements file-based planning for complex AI tasks by creating and maintaining task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md in the project workspace.
skill.summary: Plan complex AI work with persistent markdown files for goals, findings, progress, and error recovery.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, weak cryptography, network, storage, and heuristic combination findings. Review found most high and critical hits are false positives from markdown examples, headings, file names, and documentation URLs, but the skill does include real local shell hooks and scripts. No evidence found of credential theft, data exfiltration, obfuscation, or prompt injection; publish with a warning about automatic local hooks and workspace file writes.

medium_findings.title: Automatic Local Shell Hooks
medium_findings.description: The skill manifest defines command hooks that run around tool use and stop events. The commands are hardcoded and local, but community shell hooks can read workspace planning content and affect agent workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The manifest directly registers shell command hooks, and the Stop hook runs the bundled completion script. The commands do not show exfiltration, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Workspace File Writes
medium_findings.description: The initialization script creates task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md in the current project directory. This is intended behavior, but it persists task details and may store sensitive project context if users include it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly writes local markdown files when they do not already exist. The behavior is disclosed and bounded to local files, but persistence of sensitive planning content remains a real consideration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Command Blocks
low_findings.description: Most external command detections are markdown examples that show tool names such as Read, Write, WebSearch, and Edit. They are instructional text, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside markdown fences or prose examples. No executable Ruby backtick evaluation is present in these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Heuristic
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits align with normal words, headings, and markdown file references rather than cryptographic functions. No hashing or encryption implementation was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are metadata, headings, or template text. They do not contain cryptographic APIs, algorithm selection, or security-sensitive computation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URL and Browser Storage Example
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are documentation references, and the localStorage mention is an example design decision. No active network request or browser storage access is implemented by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are markdown documentation and templates. They do not execute JavaScript, fetch external resources, or transmit stored data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Manifest-Registered Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requests Bash and configures command hooks in its manifest. The reviewed commands are local and bounded, but marketplace users should be aware that enabling the skill permits automatic shell execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools and hook commands are explicit in the manifest. The absence of dynamic command construction or network transfer lowers severity.

content.user_title: Plan Complex Tasks With Persistent Files
content.value_statement: Long AI tasks can lose goals, findings, and error history across many tool calls. This skill keeps plans, discoveries, and progress in local markdown files so work can resume with clear context.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to create task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md for complex work.
Provides markdown templates for phases, decisions, errors, findings, and progress logs.
Includes scripts to initialize planning files and check whether plan phases are complete.
Defines hooks that remind the agent to inspect and update the plan during file and shell work.
Documents error recovery rules that discourage repeating the same failed action.
limitations: It does not perform project management outside local markdown files.
It may add planning overhead for simple one-step requests.
It depends on user or agent discipline to keep the files accurate.
Its shell hooks can interrupt workflows if task_plan.md is missing or incomplete.

use_cases.title: Coordinate multi-step coding work
use_cases.description: Break a feature, bug fix, or refactor into phases with visible status, decisions, and verification notes.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using Claude, Codex, or Claude Code

use_cases.title: Preserve research findings
use_cases.description: Capture sources, browser observations, and decisions in markdown before context windows become crowded.

use_cases.title: Resume long agent sessions
use_cases.description: Use progress and findings files to restore task state after pauses, tool-heavy work, or context resets.
use_cases.target_user: Power users running long AI workflows

prompt_templates.title: Start a planning session
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a complex task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use planning-with-files for this task. Create the planning files in this project, then outline phases before starting implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Track research discoveries
prompt_templates.scenario: Research workflow with web or document review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the findings file to capture key discoveries after each research pass. Keep source links, assumptions, and unanswered questions visible.

prompt_templates.title: Recover from repeated errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the three-strike error protocol. Log each failed attempt, change the next approach, and update the plan before continuing.

prompt_templates.title: Run a full project workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Manage this project with task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md. Re-read the plan before major decisions and update completion status after every phase.

output_examples.input: Start a complex bug fix with planning files.
output_examples.output: Creates a task plan with discovery, root-cause analysis, implementation, testing, and delivery phases. Adds findings and progress files for notes, decisions, and errors.

output_examples.input: Research options for a new feature.
output_examples.output: Records requirements, source links, technical decisions, and open questions in the findings file. Updates the plan as research narrows the implementation approach.

output_examples.input: Resume work after a long session.
output_examples.output: Reads the plan, findings, and progress files to identify the current phase, completed work, remaining tasks, and unresolved errors.

best_practices: Use this skill for tasks with several phases, many tool calls, or meaningful research.
Keep planning files in the project workspace so they remain with the related work.
Log errors and decisions immediately so later steps have reliable context.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for simple answers or tiny edits where planning files add noise.
Do not store secrets, credentials, or private customer data in planning files.
Do not let the plan become stale after implementation or verification changes.

faq.question: What files does this skill create?
faq.answer: It guides creation of task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md in the current project directory.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it send data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found of active data exfiltration. The skill includes web-capable tools and documentation links, so users should still review network use.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses Bash hooks and local scripts. They appear bounded, but automatic shell execution deserves a marketplace warning.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it for quick questions, one-file edits, or situations where persistent task notes could expose sensitive information.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The markdown templates are plain text and can be adapted to match a team workflow.
